content: wa.me links (https://wa.me/<countrycode+number, digits only>?text=<encoded>) are the canonical cross-platform format working on iOS, Android, desktop, and web without needing the whatsapp:// or intent:// schemes (those are reserved for in-app deep linking only). The message text must be passed through encodeURIComponent (not encodeURI) to correctly escape accented Portuguese characters and reserved symbols. Render the link as a real <a href> anchor (not a programmatic window.open) for best compatibility inside in-app browsers (Instagram/Facebook webviews) which can block JS-triggered popups; Instagram's in-app browser had a known bug failing to hand off to WhatsApp, fixed as of Instagram v354 (Oct 2024), but older client versions in the wild justify anchor-tag-first implementation and explicit manual testing.
